repo_purpose: Shinobi: Windows-native autonomous AI agent (v1.0.0) for Spanish-speaking YouTubers. Researches, transcribes, analyzes video formats, and generates localization briefs through coordinated tool execution and screen-action loops.
architecture_summary:
## Overview
Shinobi is a TypeScript/Node.js CLI agent targeting Windows x64, packaged as a single executable. It combines autonomous task coordination, browser automation (Playwright), screen interaction (nut-js), local SQLite-vec storage, and OpenAI integration.

## Layers
- **Entry/Coordinator (`src/coordinator`)**: Orchestrates missions and tool usage.
- **Tools & Skills (`src/tools`, `src/skills`)**: Discrete agent capabilities.
- **Cloud/Bridge (`src/cloud`, `src/bridge`)**: External service & IPC integration.
- **Persistence (`src/db`, `src/persistence`, `src/memory`)**: SQLite-backed state and vector memory.
- **Runtime & Utils (`src/runtime`, `src/utils`)**: Kill-switch (PowerShell ESC detection), permissions/path validation, command execution.
- **Watchers (`src/watchers`)**: Polls GitHub for upstream Hermes releases with persisted state.
- **Notifications (`src/notifications`)**: User-facing CLI notices.
- **Scripts (`scripts/`)**: Ad-hoc smoke/integration runners for `/read` and `/self` reader subsystems (referenced via `src/reader/*`).
- **Missions (`missions/`)**: Mission configuration directory (contents unverified).

## Build & Distribution
Uses esbuild + a custom `build_sea.mjs` to produce a Windows SEA binary; bundles `better-sqlite3` native `.node` files. No CI/CD detected; rebuilds via `rebuild.cmd`.

modules.name: coordinator
modules.responsibility: Mission orchestration and tool dispatch for autonomous agent loops

modules.name: notifications
modules.responsibility: CLI user notification formatting and delivery

modules.name: watchers
modules.responsibility: Polls GitHub API for Hermes releases with state persistence

modules.name: missions
modules.responsibility: Mission configuration files (contents unverified due to scan timeout)

modules.name: scripts
modules.responsibility: Smoke/integration test runners for reader CLI (/read, /self)

risks.description: License conflict in package.json: MIT in header but ISC in license field — must be resolved before redistribution.
runner.ts run_command uses execSync without invoking isDangerousCommand; command injection risk if callers don't sanitize.
missions/ directory unreadable: subagent timed out at 90s on all scans; structure and contents unverified.
docs/ sub-report unreadable (validation failed twice); documentation coverage cannot be assessed.
misc/ sub-report unreadable (validation failed twice on concerns format); contents unverified.
permissions.ts falls back to process.cwd() when WORKSPACE_ROOT unset, potentially allowing unintended filesystem access.
Windows-only lock-in: pkg targets win-x64, kill_switch uses PowerShell, hermes_watcher hardcodes APPDATA fallback.
No automated test framework or CI/CD; tests are manual ts-node scripts with console-based pass/fail counting.
Hardcoded absolute user paths (C:\Users\angel\...) in scripts/a4_run.ts break portability.
kill_switch PowerShell subprocess spawned without timeout/cleanup guarantee; potential zombie processes.
hermes_watcher.checkOnce persists state even on partial GitHub API failure, risking inconsistent state.
a4_run.ts silently swallows JSON parse errors with empty catch blocks, masking real failures.
